title: Delicate Fluffy Peruvian Shrimp Chupe De Camarones
description: A sour and fluffy Peruvian dish featuring honey and shrimp, kid-friendly and easy to make, this chupe de camarones is a twist on traditional recipes, omitting onions and garlic for a unique flavor profile.
instructions: In a large pot, bring the chicken broth to a boil over high heat.
Add the diced potatoes to the pot and cook until they are tender, about 10 minutes.
In a small bowl, whisk together the cornstarch and a splash of the chicken broth until smooth.
Add the cornstarch mixture to the pot and stir to combine.
Reduce the heat to medium and add the shrimp to the pot, cooking until they are pink and fully cooked, about 5 minutes.
In a small bowl, whisk together the honey, lemon juice, and eggs.
Temper the egg mixture by slowly pouring it into the pot, whisking constantly.
Add the milk, salt, and pepper to the pot and stir to combine.
Continue to cook the mixture over medium heat, stirring constantly, until it thickens and emulsifies, about 5 minutes.
Serve the chupe de camarones hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
